Tags: Granddaughter, Daughter, Wedding, Wife, Marriage, Code, Family, Communication
Story Summary: Miles shares a story about his granddaughter "Boppy" who has a secret family code. If he were in ICU and she whispered "Boppy wo", his eyes would light up and he'd say "bring her in". This was a secret family code.
Spiritual Metaphor: Jesus used first-century cultural "codes" that the religious leaders immediately recognized as claims of divinity.

Illustration Name: The Secret Event at Sports Arena
Tags: Sports Arena, Tony Robbins, The Secret, Criticism, Pharisees, Outreach, San Diego
Story Summary: Miles speaks about when he was invited to speak at "The Secret" (a secular motivational event at San Diego Sports Arena) and preached the gospel. Religious "Pharisees" criticized him on social media for being on the poster.
Spiritual Metaphor: Like Jesus, we must bring our light into dark, secular spaces rather than retreating out of fear of criticism.

Illustration Name: His Wife's COVID & The White Dove
Tags: Wife, Marriage, COVID, Sickness, Hospital, Dove, Prayer, Holy Spirit, Healing
Story Summary: During COVID, Miles' wife got sick for 12 days and feared she'd die. He yelled his credentials at the curb doctor to get her help. During her sickness and recovery, a white dove landed on his prayer window every single day at dusk on clockwork, bringing peace.
Spiritual Metaphor: The Holy Spirit brings comfort and presence during seasons of total crisis.

Tags: Trinity, Father, Son, Holy Spirit, Speech, Breath, Words
Story Summary: Human speech is a metaphor for the Trinity: the Will to speak is the Father, the spoken words are the Son, and the Breath required to carry the words is the Holy Spirit. Spacing and breathing must agree.
Spiritual Metaphor: The Father, Son, and Holy Spirit work in absolute unity and agreement.

Tags: Football, Weight Room, Boise State, Dumbbells, Curls, Resistance, Strength
Story Summary: Miles visited BSU weight room and saw 150-pound dumbbells. He notes that to get physically stronger, you need weights and physical resistance.
Spiritual Metaphor: When we pray for spiritual strength, God provides trials and difficult situations to serve as the "resistance" to build our faith.

Tags: Deer, Interstate 15, Freeway, San Diego, Car pool, Roadkill, Running over
Story Summary: Miles saw a deer trying to cross the 14-lane I-15 freeway in San Diego. It had no traffic light or crossing guard and had to jump multiple high concrete walls in active traffic.
Spiritual Metaphor: Trying to navigate life without God is like that deer trying to run across 14 lanes of highway traffic. It leads to spiritual roadkill.

Illustration Name: The Lion Feeding in the Pen
Tags: Lion, Feeding, Pen, Persecution, Roman, Compromise, Commitment
Story Summary: Miles describes feeding six full-grown lions custard from his hands in a six-acre pen. He contrasts this with first-century Christians being fed to lions in Rome, refusing to deny Christ even unto death, while modern believers compromise over a simple Instagram comment.
Spiritual Metaphor: True commitment to Christ persists under trial. We must find the courage to stand for our faith.

Tags: Cocaine, Addiction, Birthday, Surrender, Wife, Marriage, Cheating, Opportunity, Healing, Drugs
Story Summary: Miles recounts his drug addiction, smoking marijuana daily since age 16, and permanent deliverance 42 years ago on April 12, 1984. He shares a conversation explaining that living in sin isn't "cheating God"—it's cheating yourself out of the best life He has for you.
Spiritual Metaphor: God's grace is ready to "reverse the curse" of our addictions and mistakes whenever we surrender.

Tags: Spanish, Second-guessing, Truth, Understanding, Mindset, Doubts, Accusations
Story Summary: While learning Spanish, Miles realized he often second-guesses himself, doubting his ability to understand what is being said, though he got it right the first time.
Spiritual Metaphor: The devil whispers doubts to make us second-guess God's voice, creating a self-fulfilling prophecy (as a man thinks, so is he). We must trust God's truth.

Illustration Name: Doing the Weather on KUSI News
Tags: Weatherman, Green screen, KUSI, Weather, Authority, Sovereignty, Rent, Worry
Story Summary: Miles shares a lighthearted dream of being a weatherman on local San Diego TV, pointing out how simple it is in 70-degree weather. He contrasts human attempts to control the weather with God's effortless, sovereign authority.
Spiritual Metaphor: If God holds the vast universe, we can trust Him to handle our temporal worries (like rent).

Tags: Birth, Grandmother, Father, Puppy, Childbirth, Pain, Suffering
Story Summary: Miles' grandmother described giving birth to his father as simple as "boop" with no labor pain.
Spiritual Metaphor: Childbirth pain and physical suffering entered the world as a direct result of the Fall (Genesis 3).

Illustration Name: The Stick Figure & The Alien Cartoon (Reverse the Curse)
Tags: Cartoon, Alien, Human, Pride, Submission, Crucifixion, Rejection
Story Summary: Miles shares a cartoon depicting a human and an alien. The alien says they love Jesus and give him cookies when he visits, then asks what the human did when Jesus visited. The human answers: "We killed him."
Spiritual Metaphor: We must stop "killing" Jesus' influence in our lives through our pride, and instead submit to Him.

Tags: Conference, Prayer, Sleep-in, Texting, Discipline, Community
Story Summary: Miles met a guy at a hotel conference and agreed to pray. They texted back and forth about sleep schedules, eventually praying at 5:00 AM after the guy asked to "sleep in."
Spiritual Metaphor: Highlights the active discipline of seeking God with your whole heart and making time for connection.

Tags: Height, Chargers, Lakers, El Centro, Mission Valley, Reality, Boundaries
Story Summary: Miles notes that he is actually 5'11 and 7/8, though he was listed as 6 feet during football. He uses this to show that reality has distinct boundaries.
Spiritual Metaphor: All physical reality has absolute, defined boundaries and hierarchies established by God.

Illustration Name: Home Depot Tall Ladder & Cold Weather
Tags: Home Depot, Ladder, Tall, Cold, Temperature, Hierarchy, Boundaries
Story Summary: To buy a "tall ladder" or define "cold weather," we must operate on a comparative hierarchy starting from a baseline (the floor or freezing).
Spiritual Metaphor: All reality operates in a structured hierarchy with a beginning, middle, and end.

Illustration Name: The Lifespan of a Man & John Wayne
Tags: John Wayne, Lifespan, Richard Pryor, Cancer, Age, Mortality, Boundaries
Story Summary: The average lifespan of a man is 76.4 years, meaning Miles has exactly nine years left. He references Richard Pryor's story of John Wayne yelling at cancer to "get the heck out of here."
Spiritual Metaphor: Our physical bodies have finite timelines, but God exists on both sides of our lifespan boundaries.

Illustration Name: Moving Boston Snow vs. San Diego Weather
Tags: Boston, Snow, San Diego, Weather, Seasons, Flip-flops, Shorts
Story Summary: A pastor friend in Boston spent $150,000 just clearing snow out of his parking lot, while Miles was driving in San Diego in 90-degree weather wearing shorts and flip-flops.
Spiritual Metaphor: Environmental seasons operate under a hierarchy easily sustained and balanced by God.

Tags: Granddaughter, Boo boo, Band-aid, Tears, Play, Pantry, Attention, Medical kit, Doctor, Family
Story Summary: Miles' 2-year-old granddaughter slipped and cried, claiming her knee was bleeding though nothing was there. She led him directly to the pantry where the band-aids are kept, doing a happy dance once she got what she wanted.
Spiritual Metaphor: God wants us to bring our emotional and spiritual "boo boos" to Him. He is a patient Father ready to hold us and provide comfort.

Illustration Name: The Navy Seal on the Plane (Sean)
Tags: Plane, Oklahoma City, Navy Seal, Beard, Grandson, Texting, Prayer, Relationship, Communication
Story Summary: Miles sat next to a tough, quiet Navy Seal on a flight. After starting a conversation, they exchanged numbers, and the Seal ended up texting him weekly, researching his sick grandson's condition, and praying for him.
Spiritual Metaphor: Communication is what transforms a silent encounter into a relationship. Prayer is our active communication with God.

Tags: Leadership, 360 evaluation, Evaluation, Criticism, Sins, Mirror, Laver, Confession, Humility
Story Summary: Miles describes the brutality of reading anonymous 360-degree leadership evaluations, and the urge to deny flaws. He connects this to the bronze laver in the tabernacle, which was made of mirrors to help the priest see his reflection as he washed.
Spiritual Metaphor: Confession requires us to look into God's mirror and be completely honest about our own unorganized hearts, receiving His cleansing instead of defending our pride.

Tags: Childbirth, Labor, Wife, Sickness, Three children, Sins, Pain, Women, Strength, Marriage
Story Summary: Miles' wife's labor times got progressively longer for their three children (12, 24, and 49 hours), and he joked that she had sin in her life. In reality, he realized he could never endure that pain and has immense respect for the strength of women.
Spiritual Metaphor: We must look beyond our own complaints to see the massive burdens others carry, actively praying for them and bearing their loads to fulfill the law of Christ.

Tags: Jabez, Prayer, Blessing, Expansion, Territory, Pain, Ego, Intent
Story Summary: Jabez prayed to be blessed and have his territory expanded, not to show off, but so he would not cause pain to anyone, and God blessed him.
Spiritual Metaphor: Our prayers for blessing must be rooted in a pure desire to glorify God and protect others from pain, rather than serving our own ego.

Tags: Mother, Sickness, Death, Mortician, Wrapping, Linen, Face, Folded, Grieving, Hope
Story Summary: Miles watched a mortician respectfully wrap his deceased mother's body in a linen sheet, asking permission for each part until he reached her face, which Miles realized was the final pronouncement of death. He contrasts this with Jesus folding His headpiece separately, declaring He is not finished.
Spiritual Metaphor: The folded headpiece declares that Jesus' work is not finished. His face is not covered in death; He is alive, active, and continues to speak and minister to us.

Tags: Coma, Easter, Miracle, Roman, Guards, Stone, Resurrected, Coma theory, Cray-cray
Story Summary: Miles mocks the theory that Jesus just fell into a coma. Waking up in a freezing tomb, wrapped in heavy linen sheets, pushing a 2,000-pound stone away, and overcoming Roman guards alone while recovering is "cray-cray" (crazy).
Spiritual Metaphor: Jesus' death was verified by Roman experts, and His resurrection was a genuine, undeniable supernatural miracle, not a medical fluke.

Tags: Lazarus, Resurrection, Martha, Grave clothes, Bound, Unraveling, Mortality, Death
Story Summary: When Jesus raised Lazarus, Lazarus came out bound hand and foot and had to be unraveled by others. Jesus, however, did not need anyone to loose Him; He walked out of His grave clothes on His own power.
Spiritual Metaphor: While other biblical resurrections were temporary resuscitations where the person remained bound by mortality, Jesus' resurrection was a permanent, glorious victory over death where He walked out of His grave clothes on His own power.

Tags: TV news, Wrinkles, Getting old, Decaying, Resurrected body, Wife, Marriage
Story Summary: After Miles was featured on the local news, his wife remarked that he looked old and she saw his wrinkles. He notes that our natural bodies are decaying, but our resurrected bodies will be eternal and glorious.
Spiritual Metaphor: Our physical, natural bodies are decaying and getting old, but when we are resurrected in Christ, we will receive an eternal, glorious spiritual body that never decays.

Tags: Owl, Hooting, Calling out, Neighborhood, Wild owl
Story Summary: Miles hears a wild owl hooting every morning and tries to call back to it, but he never gets any response.
Spiritual Metaphor: We often try to find our own ways to call out to the world, but God is the only one who answers our deepest spiritual calls.

Illustration Name: The Stick Figure & The Alien Cartoon (Empty Changes Everything)
Tags: Cartoon, Alien, Human, Pride, Submission, Crucifixion, Rejection
Story Summary: Miles shares a cartoon depicting a human and an alien. The alien says they love Jesus and give him cookies when he visits, then asks what the human did when Jesus visited. The human answers: "We killed him."
Spiritual Metaphor: We must stop "killing" Jesus' influence in our lives through our pride, and instead submit to Him.

Tags: Proposal, Ring, Cubic Zirconia, Bling, Marriage, Wife, Promiser, Trust, Family
Story Summary: Miles describes proposing with a tiny 0.00001 carat cubic zirconia ring because he was broke. His wife fainted in excitement. The promise of marriage was only as good as the character of the one making it.
Spiritual Metaphor: God's promises are perfect because He is a perfect Promiser who cannot lie.

Tags: Prison, Pelican Bay, Supermax, Guards, Bulletproof, Recidivism, Heart, Sin, Reform
Story Summary: Miles visited Pelican Bay Supermax prison where guards wear bulletproof vests because isolated inmates make zip-guns out of paper clips and rubber bands. Despite high security, 53% return within three years.
Spiritual Metaphor: Human punishment or education cannot fix a corrupt heart. Only the Gospel can truly change our hearts.

Illustration Name: Developing a Friendship with a Liar
Tags: Friend, Liar, Truth, Trust, Integrity, Prophecy
Story Summary: Miles describes starting a friendship with a man who misrepresented the truth twice, making it impossible to continue the relationship.
Spiritual Metaphor: We need a God who is 100% reliable. God is holy, perfect, and keeps every promise with verified precision.

Tags: Bruce Lee, Death, Rumor, Resurrected, Witnesses, Truth
Story Summary: In 1975, when martial arts star Bruce Lee died, a rumor circulated that he would rise from the dead. It never happened, unlike Jesus' resurrection which is verified.
Spiritual Metaphor: Humans create hopeful rumors that fail, but Jesus' resurrection was a verified reality of fulfilled prophecy.

Illustration Name: Dan and Ann at Youth Group
Tags: Dan, Ann, Youth Group, Encounter, Marriage, Relationship, Greetings
Story Summary: When Miles was a youth pastor, a simple greeting at youth group between Dan and Ann led to a 40-year marriage.
Spiritual Metaphor: Simple encounters can change your life. We must never take brief, friendly greetings for granted.

Tags: Charlie Kirk, Van Jones, CNN, Text, Civil Conversation, Peace, Unity, Conflict
Story Summary: The day before Charlie Kirk died suddenly, he texted his opponent Van Jones to meet, speak, and disagree gentlemanlike. Van Jones saw the text after Charlie's death and was deeply moved.
Spiritual Metaphor: We are called to rise above cultural divisions, have civil conversations, and love those we disagree with.

Tags: Hunting, Woods, Monster, Brother, Advesary, Unity, Deception, Perspective
Story Summary: A hunter saw a terrifying "monster" in the woods, dodging behind trees. When the monster finally stepped out right in front of him, he realized it was his own brother.
Spiritual Metaphor: The enemy deceives us into seeing those who look or think differently as "monsters," when they are actually brothers and sisters in Christ.

Tags: Grim Reaper, Sickle, Death, Reaping, Sowing, Harvest, Consequence
Story Summary: The term "reap" is connected to the Grim Reaper, who carries a large sickle to cut down and harvest souls.
Spiritual Metaphor: Reaping is a natural and spiritual law of harvesting what you have planted. We always reap what we sow.

Tags: Son, Navy Seal, Pool, Hands tied, Deep end, Push, Safety, Trial, Strength, Father
Story Summary: Miles played a game where he tied his young son's hands, threw him in the pool, and pushed him away from the side to build his strength, ready to save him if needed.
Spiritual Metaphor: A loving father pushes his child past their comfort zone to build strength. God does the same to build our faith.

Tags: Apple, Seed, Multiplication, Tree, Harvest, Generosity, Math
Story Summary: An apple seed grows into a tree producing 300-600 apples annually, yielding over 567 million apples after 30 years of planting seeds.
Spiritual Metaphor: We can count the seeds in an apple, but we cannot count the apples in a seed. Every act of generosity has an infinite, multiplicative harvest.

Tags: Ice Cube, Grind, 5 Years, Discipline, Entitlement, Success, Work
Story Summary: Miles quotes rapper Ice Cube: "If you're not willing to work five years and grind it out... you don't deserve it."
Spiritual Metaphor: We expect overnight success, but receiving God's blessings and fulfilling your purpose requires long-term, disciplined planting.

Tags: Abraham, Melchizedek, Sodom, Tenth, Tithe, Grace, Honor, Victory
Story Summary: Abraham voluntarily gave Melchizedek (priest-king of Salem) a tenth of his victory spoils out of gratitude, while refusing to take anything from the corrupt King of Sodom.
Spiritual Metaphor: Tithing was established by grace and gratitude long before the law was written, as an act of honor to God as our source.

Tags: Stolen, Car, Church, Thief, Police, Restored, Security, Trust
Story Summary: Miles' car was stolen from the church parking lot during a service, but returned within an hour after the thief ran a stoplight right in front of a police officer.
Spiritual Metaphor: When we surrender everything to God, telling Him: "All I have is yours," He protects and manages our security according to His sovereign plan.

Tags: Children, Pantry, Cookies, Chocolate, Drool, Stealing, Robbing God, Integrity
Story Summary: A pastor friend caught his children in the pantry with cheeks stuffed and chocolate drool on their faces, while they denied eating cookies.
Spiritual Metaphor: Many Christians claim they aren't robbing God, yet they walk around with the "chocolate drool" of withheld tithes and uncommitted resources in their pockets.

Illustration Name: Taking a Youth Pastor Job for $500/Month
Tags: Chargers, Paycheck, Youth Pastor, Mortgage, Bills, Trust, Faithfulness, Money
Story Summary: After retiring from the Chargers making $9,000/game, God called Miles to be a youth pastor for $500/month, though his bills were $1,500. He chose to trust God, and spent three years seeing Him faithfully provide.
Spiritual Metaphor: God wants to make us holy and teach us to trust Him. If He can trust us to be faithful with our money, He can trust us with His kingdom's power.

Tags: Brain, Neurons, Synapses, AMCC, Willpower, Discipline, Sickness, Training, Suffer, Mindset
Story Summary: The Anterior Mid-Cingulate Cortex (AMCC) is a two-and-a-half-ounce brain structure responsible for willpower that physically grows \*only\* when you do things you do not want to do.
Spiritual Metaphor: Obeying God and pushing through spiritual resistance (like fasting or prayer) physically builds our spiritual willpower and longsuffering (James 1:2).

Tags: Scrips Ranch, Jacuzzi, Millionaire, Homeless, Fear, Lies, ANTs, Mindset
Story Summary: Miles sat in a jacuzzi with a wealthy man who owned a 3,200-sq-ft home but confessed a constant fear of ending up homeless, despite having friends who would easily help him.
Spiritual Metaphor: The devil is a master liar who whispers ridiculous, unfounded "automatic negative thoughts" (ANTs) into our heads to paralyze us with fear.

Tags: REM sleep, Dreams, Paralysis, Half-awake, Fear, Captive, Thoughts, Mindset
Story Summary: During REM sleep, our brains paralyze our physical muscles so we don't act out our dreams. Sometimes, waking up half-awake in this state causes a terrifying feeling of paralysis.
Spiritual Metaphor: We must take active command of our thoughts. When unbiblical thoughts enter our minds, we must "take every thought captive" and tell our minds to align with God's truth.

Tags: The Third Option, Race, Book, Intervies, CNN, TMZ, Fear, Peace, Trust
Story Summary: When writing his book on race, the devil whispered to Miles weekly that he would destroy his church. By preparing his heart and trusting God, his heart was at peace during 100 intense interviews.
Spiritual Metaphor: Trusting God does not mean difficult circumstances will disappear, but it changes your heart so you are prepared to stand strong in the fire.

Illustration Name: The Shadow of the Big Truck
Tags: Truck, Shadow, Worry, Fear, Testimony, Control, Trust, Mindset
Story Summary: Miles describes seeing a giant shadow of a truck sweep over his car, making him think he was going to get hit, until a quiet voice told him it was just a shadow.
Spiritual Metaphor: We are often terrified of the \*shadow\* of death (financial worry, relationship conflict, health struggles) rather than death itself.

Tags: Dishwasher, Dishes, Grease, Cleaning, Surrender, Pride, Grace, Family, Wife
Story Summary: Miles was confused when his wife told him to rinse dishes before putting them in the dishwasher.
Spiritual Metaphor: We think we need to clean ourselves up before coming to God. But He is the dishwasher—He cleans us, we just need to surrender.

Tags: Father, Child, Bookshelf, Reaching, Standards, Ransom, Salvation, Grace, Family
Story Summary: A little boy tried desperately to reach a book on the very top of a bookshelf but was too short. His father walked in and reached it effortlessly.
Spiritual Metaphor: The standards of Heaven require absolute perfection, and no matter how hard we try, we cannot reach it. So God the Father came down to us in Jesus Christ.

Tags: Daniel, Nebuchadnezzar, Dream, Astrologers, Magicians, Plan, Purpose
Story Summary: Nebuchadnezzar demanded his astrologers tell him both what he dreamed and what it meant. Only Daniel, trusting God, prayed and received the answer.
Spiritual Metaphor: Unlike false gods, our God is a personal, active, and conversational helper who has a specific, structured plan for our lives.

Tags: Frog, Kettle, Boiling, Temperature, Deception, Mindset, Culture
Story Summary: A frog in cold water on a stove will stay and boil to death because the temperature increases so gradually it doesn't recognize the danger.
Spiritual Metaphor: The devil masterfully, gradually deceptions our cultural leaders, normalizing moral decay so slowly that we don't realize the spiritual danger until it is too late.

Illustration Name: The Dallas Airport & School Board Meeting
Tags: Dallas, School board, Activist, Pornography, Kids, Ministry, Outreach
Story Summary: An activist showed Miles a children's cartoon book from a school library containing explicit pornography, highlighting the battle for the next generation.
Spiritual Metaphor: Represents the urgent spiritual battle facing the next generation. Our children are targeted, and only the Gospel can truly change their hearts.

Tags: College, Chargers, Rams, NFL, Draft, Flyers, Scouts, Trust, Plan, Career
Story Summary: Miles, from a small school in Connecticut, was stressed for months before being drafted, cut, and eventually landing with the San Diego Chargers.
Spiritual Metaphor: We believe a specific person or circumstance holds our future, but our hope and future are held solely by God.

Tags: Youth Group, Pregnant, Teenager, Confusion, Virgin Birth, Miracle, Holy Spirit
Story Summary: A pregnant high school girl in Miles' church claimed she didn't know how it happened, which Miles laughed at.
Spiritual Metaphor: Highlights the contrast of Mary's virgin birth, which was a genuine, supernatural impossibility achieved solely by the Holy Spirit.

Tags: Golf, North County, 16th Hole, Warm-up, Driving Range, Swing, Rhythm, Discipline, Consistency
Story Summary: Running late for a golf game, Miles played the final three holes without a warm-up, hitting the ball horribly, before going to the driving range to get his swing back.
Spiritual Metaphor: You cannot just "warm-up" with God on Sundays and expect a powerful spiritual life; walking by faith requires daily, consistent rhythm.

Tags: Montana, Whitefish, Cold Plunge, Freezing Lake, Key card, Community, Endurance
Story Summary: In Whitefish, Montana, Miles stood in a freezing 39-degree natural lake for 15 minutes because he was with a group of 15 other trainers doing it together.
Spiritual Metaphor: Doing things in community distracts us from the physical pain of trials, encouraging us to go much further than we ever would on our own.

Tags: Conference, sdpd, Faith community, Gravity, Oxygen, Love, Trust, Perspective
Story Summary: Miles challenged a moderator's label of "faith community" by pointing out that everyone has faith—in gravity, oxygen, money, or love.
Spiritual Metaphor: We are all designed to live by faith; the only question is where you put your trust.

Illustration Name: Abraham and Sarah "Good as Dead" Childbirth
Tags: Abraham, Sarah, Laughed, Baby, Childbirth, Age, Mortality, Miracle, Covenant, Wife, Marriage
Story Summary: God promised Abraham (100) and Sarah (90) a baby. Though Abraham was "as good as dead" physically, they trusted God and a massive lineage was born.
Spiritual Metaphor: Out of biological impossibility and mortal limits, God brings infinite life and legacy to those who trust His covenant.

Tags: Richard Pryor, Late Show, Club, Entitlement, Effort, Expectation, Fasting, Discipline, Pride
Story Summary: Miles references Richard Pryor's comedy skit about people who show up late but expect to get in, comparing it to Christians who expect blessings without spiritual effort.
Spiritual Metaphor: Blessings are not about entitlement; we must actively seek and pursue them through prayer and fasting.

Tags: NFL, Workshop, Evangelist, Calling, Empty Room, Preparation, Attention, Career
Story Summary: Over 30 years ago, Miles was the only player who showed up for an NFL conference workshop on evangelism, gaining the speaker's full attention and clarifying his calling.
Spiritual Metaphor: What seems like an insignificant, empty room can be the exact place where God sharpens your primary spiritual calling.

Sermon Title: We Need To Talk: The Holy Spirit
Tags: Texas, Partners, Construction, Vision, Negotiator, Project Manager, Father, Son, Holy Spirit, Trinity
Story Summary: A Texas construction company had three equal partners: the visionary, the negotiator, and the project manager who did the physical building.
Spiritual Metaphor: This models the Trinity: the Father plans, the Son negotiates/signs the contract, and the Holy Spirit does the work of internal transformation.

Sermon Title: We Need To Talk: The Holy Spirit
Tags: NYPD, Detective, Decoy, Surveillance, Father, Ride-along, Child, Protection, Battle, Security, Family
Story Summary: Miles' father, an NYPD detective, took him as a decoy on ride-alongs and had him walk down alleys to check suspects, promising he was right behind him.
Spiritual Metaphor: When the Holy Spirit leads us into difficult assignments or spiritual battles, our loving Heavenly Father is always positioned right behind us, securing our victory.

Tags: Football, Grandson, Routes, Out of Breath, Reality, Illusion, Pride, Family
Story Summary: Miles tried to run routes with his 9-year-old grandson, believing he still had his NFL-level speed, but was completely out of breath in five routes.
Spiritual Metaphor: We frequently hold a false, inflated perception of our own goodness or strength. We need God's presence to show us our true reality.

Tags: Rats, Water, Swimming, Hope, Survival, Rescue, Fainting, Lifegiving
Story Summary: Rats treading water fainted and began sinking at 15 minutes. But after being rescued, dried, and rested, they swam for 60 hours in a second test because they held hope of a rescue.
Spiritual Metaphor: Hope is a powerful, lifegiving force. Jesus is our eternal hope born to rescue us from our spiritual drowning.

Tags: Boston, Pizza, Flight, Sickness, Wife, COVID, Prayer, Friendship, Faithfulness, Marriage
Story Summary: While Miles' wife was hospitalized with severe COVID, his close friend Matthew flew from Boston to San Diego just to deliver pizza and pray with him every three hours.
Spiritual Metaphor: God expresses His absolute faithfulness and love through the sacrificial actions of His people. A true friend is willing to go to extreme lengths to carry your burden.

Sermon Title: Smell The Roses – How To Love Yourself!
Tags: Restaurant, Hands, Birthmark, Mirror, Self-Worth, Identity, Appreciating, Business, Reflection
Story Summary: Sitting in a restaurant, Miles began studying his own hands and birthmark, realizing we get so busy in our routines that we forget to appreciate how God uniquely made us.
Spiritual Metaphor: Loving yourself is a holy, biblical act of appreciating God's design in you and realizing you are fearfully and wonderfully made in His image.

Sermon Title: Smell The Roses – How To Love Yourself!
Tags: Talents, Money, Parable, Burying, Fear, Faith, Abundance, Stewardship
Story Summary: In the Parable of the Talents, two servants invested their talents and doubled their return, while the third buried his talent in the ground out of fear and lost everything.
Spiritual Metaphor: God has given each of us distinct natural and spiritual talents. Burying them out of fear of failure brings spiritual torment, but investing them out of faith opens the door to infinite joy.

Tags: Hotel, Rock Climbing, Crevices, Strongholds, Lies, Claws, Mindset, Handhold
Story Summary: Miles describes climbing an artificial wall and realizing he must grab and step on tiny crevices designed to hold him. He compares this to the devil finding half-truths to latch lies onto.
Spiritual Metaphor: The devil latching lies to half-truths to build strongholds, which we must take captive.

Illustration Name: The Scripps Ranch Millionaire's Homeless Fear
Tags: Scripps Ranch, Jacuzzi, Millionaire, Homeless, Fear, Lies, ANTs, Mindset
Story Summary: Miles sat in a jacuzzi with a wealthy man who owned a 3,200-sq-ft home but confessed a constant fear of ending up homeless, despite having friends who would easily help him.
Spiritual Metaphor: The devil is a master liar who whispers ridiculous, unfounded "automatic negative thoughts" (ANTs) to paralyze us with fear.

Tags: Reading, Dark Room, Light Switch, Blindness, Revelation, Wife, Sin, Marriage
Story Summary: Miles was reading in a dark room and insisted he could see fine. His wife turned on the light, and he was shocked to see how dark it actually was.
Spiritual Metaphor: We think we are good people whose lives aren't that bad, staying blind until God's light shines on our need for a Savior.

Illustration Name: The Demonic Goo Monster on the Front Pew
Tags: Preaching, Demonic Monster, Green Goo, Pew, Sister-in-law, Marriage, Fear, Victory, Satan, Wife
Story Summary: A demonic creature covered in green goo slammed into a church. The pastor calmly said he wasn't scared because he had been married to the demon's sister for 20 years.
Spiritual Metaphor: The devil and his demons try to terrorize us, but we do not need to fear because Jesus conquered them on the cross.

Illustration Name: Marshawn Lynch "I'm Just About That Action"
Tags: Seattle Seahawks, Super Bowl, Marshawn Lynch, Beast Mode, Deion Sanders, Action, Obedience, Baptism
Story Summary: Star running back Marshawn Lynch refused to talk to the press, telling Deion Sanders: "I'm just about that action."
Spiritual Metaphor: There is a massive difference between just talking about Jesus and actually living for Him out of action and obedience.

Illustration Name: "Because I Said So" & The Coffee Table
Tags: Levi, Coffee Table, Feet, Kids, Obedience, Role Model, Parenting, Family
Story Summary: Miles' son Levi put his feet on the table. Miles told him not to, and Levi pointed out Miles doing it. Miles said "Do what I say, not what I do!"
Spiritual Metaphor: Unlike hypocritical parents, Jesus never just says "because I said so." He went first and modeled everything He asks of us.

Tags: Ice Bath, Freezing, Athletes, Inflammation, Anxiety, Hesitation, Commitment, Submerge, Baptizo
Story Summary: Athletes use cold ice baths for physical benefits, but many stand at the edge hesitating because it is too cold.
Spiritual Metaphor: We want spiritual benefits (peace, joy, healing), but refuse to fully submerge ourselves (tithe, serve, share faith) into Christ's call.

Illustration Name: Grandson's "Elf on the Shelf" and Birth
Tags: Grandson, Elf on the Shelf, Reindeer, Santa, Myths, Incarnation, Eternity, Family
Story Summary: Miles' grandson asked about his elf on the shelf and if his pregnant mother could "pop out" a baby, leading to a discussion about Sarah having a baby at 90.
Spiritual Metaphor: Christmas myths distract us from the real historical truth that Jesus is an eternal God who existed before the world's foundation.

Illustration Name: Abraham's Mistress Hagar in the Desert
Tags: Abraham, Hagar, Desert, Ishmael, Barren, El Roi, God Who Sees, Mistress, Sickness, Family, Wife, Marriage
Story Summary: Abraham had a child with Hagar, but jealousy arose and Hagar fled to the desert to die. The Angel of the Lord met her there, revealing Himself as El Roi (The God Who Sees Me).
Spiritual Metaphor: God sees you and knows your deepest crisis. He is a personal, active provider who meets us in our desert when we surrender.

Tags: Fasting, Physical vs Spiritual, Self-Control, Detox
Story Summary: In football, a first down is exactly 10 yards, which is 30 feet. This is the exact length of the human digestive tract from mouth to exit.
Spiritual Metaphor: Fasting stops physical consumption in this 30-foot tube, creating a space to detoxify our bodies and spirits to focus completely on face-to-face intimacy with God.

Tags: Morning Prayer, Integrity, Seeking God, Protection
Story Summary: The tomato frog in Costa Rica leaks a white toxic sweat to defend itself after being bitten, but it is already chewed up and injured.
Spiritual Metaphor: Many Christians live like the tomato frog, seeking God only at night after being chewed up by the day's stress, instead of seeking Him first in the morning for protection.

Illustration Name: Preaching on the Road to Cincinnati
Tags: Divine Detours, Evangelism, Ministry, Orchestration
Story Summary: When a flight to Cincinnati was diverted, Pastor Miles and a friend rented a car and offered a ride to three college students. Miles turned around in the passenger seat and preached the Gospel, leading two of them to Christ. His friend realized he was called to ministry because of this divine detour.
Spiritual Metaphor: God orchestrates seemingly disruptive, chaotic detours in our lives to position us to fulfill His dream, make a difference, and step into our calling.

Tags: End in Mind, Preparation, Self-Control, Excellence
Story Summary: A friend taught Miles to park by backing into spaces. Police cars always do this because when an emergency call comes, they don't have time to back out. It deals with hassle on the front end so the back end is effortless.
Spiritual Metaphor: We must live our lives with the end in mind. Dealing with baggage and seeking God on the front end allows us to effortlessly step forward when God calls us.

Tags: Persistent Prayer, Anniversary, Intercession, San Diego
Story Summary: When the church acquired Liberty Station, Miles found an old chair, took it to the roof, and sat in it daily for years weeping and praying for San Diego.
Spiritual Metaphor: The old roof chair represents the immense power of persistent, tear-filled, and humble prayer on the front end of a long-term ministry legacy.

Sermon Stories Doc: Sermon Illustrations: My Story - I Was
Tags: Evangelism, Altar Call, Personal Story, Simplicity
Story Summary: Miles spent years witnessing to his dad with no success. While walking Miramar Lake, he asked his dad when he would get saved, and his dad replied that he was waiting for Miles to do an altar call and ask him. They sat on a bench and did it right there.
Spiritual Metaphor: The Gospel is a person, not information. Sharing your story is simply declaring what you were, how Jesus saved you, and what your life has been like since then.

Sermon Stories Doc: Sermon Illustrations: My Story - I Was
Tags: Happiness, Materialism, Idolatry, False Comforts
Story Summary: Miles' daughter saw a dress she just had to have and started hyperventilating from excitement, forcing Miles to physically pull her out of the store.
Spiritual Metaphor: We get desperately hyped over temporary material possessions, believing they are our source of happiness, when permanent joy is only found in Christ.

Sermon Title: Rock Church - Why Christmas? - Part 1, Walking in God's Favor
Sermon Stories Doc: Sermon Illustrations: Why Christmas? - Walking in God's Favor
Tags: Divine Purpose, Miracle of Life, Pregnancy, Value
Story Summary: Pregnancy starts with a single cell called a zygote, which multiplies exponentially (1 to 2 to 4 to 8, etc.) to eventually form billions of cells.
Spiritual Metaphor: Life in the womb is a divine partnership. God designed your frame and wrote your purpose before any of your days even began (Psalm 139).

Sermon Title: Rock Church - Why Christmas? - Part 1, Walking in God's Favor
Sermon Stories Doc: Sermon Illustrations: Why Christmas? - Walking in God's Favor
Tags: Obedience, Rationalization, Peter, Overthinking
Story Summary: Peter walked on water without understanding the physics of it. But when he looked at the wind and began to rationalize, he talked himself out of the miracle.
Spiritual Metaphor: Humility means obeying God even when we don't understand the "how." Overthinking and rationalization will talk us out of the miracles God has for us.

Sermon Title: When Bad Things Happen - Part 1, Dwell On The Past
Illustration Name: High School & College Regrets vs. Faithful Remembrance
Tags: Remembrance, Faith, Trials, Testimony
Story Summary: Pastor Miles shares about old friends dwelling on high school girlfriends, missed pro careers, and potential riches.
Spiritual Metaphor: He explains that while dwelling on regrets is useless, we must 'dwell on the past' in a spiritual sense to remember how God got us through previous impossible situations.

Sermon Title: When Bad Things Happen - Part 1, Dwell On The Past
Illustration Name: The $125/Week Youth Pastor Step of Faith
Tags: Trust, Finance, Leap of Faith, Provision, Career, Testimony
Story Summary: After retiring from the Chargers, Miles accepted a youth pastor job paying only $125/week ($500/month) with a family, mortgage, and cars in San Diego.
Spiritual Metaphor: He chose to trust God, who faithfully provided for three years before his paycheck caught up. This became his 'stone of remembrance' for future major projects like Liberty Station.

Sermon Title: When Bad Things Happen - Part 2, Mother's Day
Illustration Name: Saving Sister Margaret in the Pool
Tags: Family, Humorous, Sister, Support
Story Summary: Pastor Miles jokes on stage with his sister Margaret about how he 'saved her life' when she was drowning in three feet of water in their pool, walking on water to yank her out when he was six and she was three.

Sermon Title: When Bad Things Happen - Part 2, Mother's Day
Illustration Name: Laurie's Son Michael and the Foot of the Cross
Tags: Addiction, Surrender, Mother, Son, Restoration, Testimony, Family
Story Summary: Laurie shares how her son Michael was a heroin addict for 8 years, facing 5 felony counts. Angry at God, Laurie met Michael at the foot of a large cross and surrendered him, laying him at Jesus' feet.
Spiritual Metaphor: Michael got saved, attended ministry school, married, and entered full-time missions.

Sermon Title: When Bad Things Happen - Part 2, Mother's Day
Illustration Name: Teresa's Midnight Vigil and the Prison of Worry
Tags: Worry, Peace, Surrender, Mother, Son, Addiction, Health, Testimony, Family
Story Summary: Teresa shares about her son's meth addiction from age 14 to 18. Obsessed with trying to fix him, she stayed up late every night, losing weight and getting physically sick from worry.
Spiritual Metaphor: When she finally surrendered him to God, she found complete supernatural freedom and peace. Her son got clean and built a beautiful family.

Sermon Title: When Bad Things Happen - Part 5, Get Involved!
Illustration Name: The Madea Prompt and Breaking the Redline
Tags: Outreach, Comfort Zone, Redlining, Compassion, Brokenness, Community
Story Summary: Watching Tyler Perry's 'Madea Goes to Prison' where a man helps his childhood friend who became a prostitute, God challenged Miles about helping prostitutes in San Diego.
Spiritual Metaphor: Miles shares his vision to 'hire' prostitutes all at once to offer them housing, jobs, and Jesus, and compares avoidance of brokenness to historical banking 'redlining'.

Sermon Title: Worthy - Part 1, What Is God Worthy Of?
Illustration Name: Chasing the Tight End and the Excuse of Injury
Tags: Effort, Excellence, Excuses, Football, Sports, Worthiness, Accountability
Story Summary: During a Chargers defensive meeting, a coach repeatedly played a clip of a heavy tight end outrunning a fast cornerback. Asked if he was running as fast as he could, the cornerback made the excuse 'I'm hurt' to avoid being benched or admitting defeat.
Spiritual Metaphor: Miles uses this to show how we give God mediocre effort and then make excuses.

Sermon Title: Rock Church - Dialed In - Part 1, Prayer
