Sermon Title: Rock Church - Dialed In - Part 1, Prayer
Theme/Category: Morning Prayer, Integrity, Seeking God, Protection

Illustration Name: Translucent Kid Danny & The Youth Group

Sermon Title: Rock Church -  The Third Option - Part 1, The Third Option
Rock Church -  The Third Option - Part 1, The Third Option
Rock Church  - The Third Option - Part 2, Unalienable Hope
Theme/Category: Kalief Browder, Injustice, Rikers Island, Dehumanization, Labels

Sermon Title: Rock Church  - The Third Option - Part 2, Unalienable Hope
Theme/Category: Rod Carew, Heart Transplant, Reuland, Unity, One Race

Sermon Title: Relationship Goals - Lessons from the Trinity
Theme/Category: Trinity, Design, Holy Spirit, Creation, Image of God
Spiritual Metaphor: We are made in the image of God so that His Holy Spirit can live inside us. Apart from Him, we are empty and unable to fulfill our true purpose. We must let Him animate and guide us.

Sermon Title: Relationship Goals - Lessons from the Trinity
Spiritual Metaphor: Our heavenly Father sees a design and potential in us that far exceeds our limited self-perspective. We must align our self-worth with our Creator's vision rather than living in survival mode.

Sermon Title: Kingdom Warrior - Part 2, A Warrior's Identity
Theme/Category: Authority, Access, Identity, Co-heirs, Leverage, Petco Park
Spiritual Metaphor: When we accept Christ, we receive a spiritual 'ticket'—a new kingdom identity. It grants us immense authority, spiritual leverage, and access to the throne room of God as co-heirs with Christ.

Illustration Name: Healing the Atheist's Leg at the Porn Convention
Theme/Category: Evangelism, Outreach, Healing, Faith, Demonstration, Miracle
Spiritual Metaphor: God's kingdom is meant to be actively demonstrated, not just declared inside a church building. When we step out of our comfort zones, God will perform the unexplainable to challenge unbelief.

Theme/Category: Redemption, Prison, Service, Purpose, Restoration, Transformation
Spiritual Metaphor: God is able to redeem any past, no matter how rugged or broken, and repurpose anyone's hands for beautiful, unexpected works of service to build up His kingdom.

Tags: Battle, Spiritual Warfare, Race, Division
Story Summary: Trying to fight racial division in the natural realm (politics, media) is like fighting on the wrong turf where we will lose; we must fight spiritually using God's tools where His victory is assured.

Tags: Race, Diversity, Humorous, Skin Color
Story Summary: Instead of pretending to be colorblind, we should see and celebrate each other's colors and cultures, appreciating diversity as a beautiful mosaic designed by a creative God.

Tags: Family, Tradition, Humorous, Pets
Story Summary: Humorous family tradition illustrating the joy and playfulness of raising children and finding joy in chaotic domestic moments.

Tags: Incarnation, Obedience, Sacrifice, Humorous
Story Summary: Jesus voluntarily gave up His cosmic rights and heaven's luxury to move into the mess and stinky situations of our human lives to save us.

Sermon Title: Rock Church - Lord of the Things - Part 1, Getting in the Fast Lane
Tags: Fasting, Discipline
Story Summary: Fasting pauses consumption in this 30-foot tube, detoxifying the body and mind to create a vast space for face-to-face intimacy with God.

Sermon Title: Rock Church - Lord of the Things - Part 1, Getting in the Fast Lane
Tags: Detox, Spiritual Life
Story Summary: An owl swallows prey whole, digesting the meat and spitting out compressed bones and fur as a pellet. Fasting and prayer detoxify and cast out the toxic, hard-to-digest waste in our spiritual lives.

Sermon Title: Rock Church - My Story - Part 1, The Power of Your Story
Tags: Temptation, Danger
Story Summary: Entering a cage containing six full-grown lions to feed them directly. The warning that they are still wild, dangerous predators mirrors how we think we can safely "tame" or play with secret sins.

Sermon Title: Rock Church - My Story - Part 1, The Power of Your Story
Tags: Resistance, Fighting
Story Summary: The intense, focused battle stance of UFC champion Ronda Rousey when stepping into the ring, illustrating the active, aggressive stance required to fight spiritual battles and resist the devil.

Sermon Title: Rock Church - Superman - Part 1, A Prophetic Challenge
Tags: Presence, Stillness, Supernatural
Story Summary: Standing perfectly still holding a coat hanger antenna to get black-and-white TV reception, symbolizing the stillness and alignment needed to receive God's supernatural power.

Tags: Surrender, Trust
Story Summary: Tandem jumping from 12,000 feet and letting go of the bar, illustrating the complete surrender and letting go of our human safety nets to walk by faith.

Illustration Name: David's Goliath "I don't do that anymore"
Tags: Calling, Courage, Victory
Story Summary: Teenage David tells King Saul that yesterday he kept sheep, but today he is a giant killer and doesn't do sheep anymore, modeling how we must declare our new identity in Christ.

Sermon Title: On Earth As It Is In Heaven - Part 1, Supernatural Expectation
Tags: Design, Holy Spirit, Creation, Image of God, Powerless
Story Summary: We are made in the image of God so that His Holy Spirit can live inside us. Apart from Him, we are empty and unable to fulfill our true purpose. We must let Him animate and guide us.

Sermon Title: On Earth As It Is In Heaven - Part 1, Supernatural Expectation
Tags: Potential, Design, Worth, Identity, Calling
Story Summary: Our heavenly Father sees a design and potential in us that far exceeds our limited self-perspective. We must align our self-worth with our Creator's vision rather than living in survival mode.

Tags: Flesh, Compromise, Self-Control, Discipline, Boxing
Story Summary: We cannot 'spar' or compromise with our fleshly carnal nature. If we try to take it lightly or satisfy it slightly, it will swing hard and knock us out spiritually. We must completely crucify the flesh.

Tags: Fear, Terror, Spiritual Warfare, Childhood
Story Summary: The devil's tactics are designed to terrorize and paralyze us with fear. However, as believers, we do not need to cower in fear because 'greater is He who is in us than he who is in the world.'

Illustration Name: Bat Echolocation of Moths vs. Rocks
Tags: Echolocation, Heart, Pharisees, Revelation
Story Summary: Human beings look at the outward appearance, but God 'echolocates' the heart. Jesus' spiritual waves bounce off self-righteous solid 'rocks' (Pharisees) but register the fluttering, broken, and desperate 'hearts' (Zaccheus).

Tags: Witnessing, Salvation, Immediate Response, Evangelism
Story Summary: When God prompts us to receive His love and forgiveness, we shouldn't hesitate or rationalize. We should immediately jump up and receive His invitation.

Tags: Discipline, Fasting, Survival, Warrior, Training
Story Summary: Spiritual disciplines like fasting are designed to train us as 'spiritual Rambos.' By mastering and denying our physical flesh, we become disciplined warriors who can accomplish God's mission in any environment.

Tags: Baggage, Warfare, Focus, Simplicity, Sacrifice
Story Summary: When God calls us into spiritual warfare, we cannot drag our carnal 'typewriters' (fleshly habits, comfort zones, baggage) with us. We must leave them on the ground and pack light to be effective.

Tags: Miracles, Courage, Boldness, Faith, Power
Story Summary: This serves as a metaphor for the Christian life and the way believers should pursue the promises of God. To "take the promise" requires a "Woo Hit" level of commitment. It is the difference between half-hearted effort and total spiritual immersion. When a believer engages their faith with that level of intensity and power, it creates a visible, undeniable impact on the world around them. It challenges the listener to stop playing tentatively and start living with the power and conviction that God’s promises demand.

Tags: Flesh, Discipline, Persistence, Temptation
Story Summary: This illustration highlights the necessity of persistence in prayer and the boldness required to claim God's promises. Many believers ask God for something once and give up if they don't see an immediate result. Pastor Miles uses this to demonstrate that "taking the promise" often involves a continuous, confident pursuit. Just as a child persists with a loving father, we are encouraged to remain steadfast in our petitions, trusting in God's character and His timing to fulfill His word.

Sermon Title: Rock Church - What Does Wisdom Say
Illustration Name: The Queen Termite and the 30,000 Eggs
Tags: Creation, Solomon, Science, Wisdom, Wonders
Story Summary: This illustration serves as a reflection on divine design and the inherent wisdom found in creation. Just as the termite operates within its specific purpose with maximum output, wisdom dictates that individuals should understand their God-given design and fulfill their roles with diligence and consistency.

Sermon Title: Rock Church - What Does Wisdom Say
Tags: Habits, Weaning, Discipline, Stewardship, Comfort
Story Summary: This is a practical lesson in stewardship and financial wisdom. It illustrates that wisdom often requires the discipline to sacrifice immediate desires or "wants" for a greater spiritual or practical purpose. Weaning oneself from unnecessary costs is presented as an act of taking control over one’s resources to align with wiser living.

Sermon Title: Rock Church - The Reasons We Run From God
Illustration Name: Witnessing to His Father by Miramar Lake
Tags: Family, Witnessing, Altar Call, Salvation, Father
Story Summary: This illustration serves as a powerful metaphor for the urgency of evangelism within the family unit. It demonstrates that "running from God" can often be a generational or relational barrier that requires bold, loving intervention. The application focuses on the courage required to witness to those closest to us and the restorative power of God's grace when we stop running and start sharing.

Sermon Title: Rock Church - The Reasons We Run From God
Tags: Urgency, Witnessing, Tragedy, Salvation, Testimony
Story Summary: April’s journal serves as a metaphor for the internal tug-of-war between the flesh and the spirit. It highlights the transparency and sincerity required to maintain a relationship with God. The preaching application emphasizes that even in our youth or our darkest moments of running, God is listening to the honest cries of our hearts and offers a path back to hope and redemption.

Sermon Title: Rock Church - The Plan - Part 1, Remember
Illustration Name: The Orange Boundary Line at Donovan Prison
Tags: Prison, Warriors, Calling, Identity, Battle
Story Summary: The orange line serves as a powerful metaphor for God's boundaries and "The Plan" He has for our lives. Just as the inmates have a clear marker that ensures order and safety within the prison context, God provides spiritual and moral boundaries designed for our protection and purpose. When we "Remember the Plan," we are called to acknowledge these divine boundaries. Crossing the line represents a departure from God's intended path, leading to spiritual danger or a loss of freedom. The illustration encourages the congregation to respect the "orange lines" in their own lives—those clear scriptural mandates and convictions that keep them within the safety of God's will.

Sermon Title: Rock Church - Vision Sunday 2012 by Miles McPherson
Tags: Vision, Goals, Persistence, Wife, Marriage
Story Summary: A vision is a picture of your preferred future. God wants us to have a thriving, thriving-focused spiritual vision for our lives instead of a mere survival mentality.

Sermon Title: Rock Church - Why Bad Things Happen to Good People by Miles McPherson
Tags: Sin, Temptation, Danger, Wisdom
Story Summary: Secret sins we try to "tame" and play with are still wild, dangerous predators waiting to destroy us if we let them remain.

Sermon Title: Rock Church - The Fine Line - Part 1, Wake Up and Look at The Fine Line by Miles McPherson
Illustration Name: The Republican National Convention (RNC) Teleprompter Training
Tags: Worldview, Distraction, Focus, Wife, Marriage
Story Summary: We must learn to walk "the fine line," ignoring the massive distractions of the world and keeping our focus entirely on the lens of God's truth.

Sermon Title: Rock Church - The Fine Line - Part 1, Wake Up and Look at The Fine Line by Miles McPherson
Illustration Name: The Double-Pound Sign and the "In Jesus' Name" Edit
Tags: Integrity, Witnessing, Courage, Jesus
Story Summary: The world will try to edit and water down our Christian testimony to make it non-offensive. We must remain bold and proclaim the name of Jesus without compromise.

Tags: Character, Suffering, Trials, Testing
Story Summary: Peaceful times don't test our true spiritual standing. The hot water of trials, suffering, and sacrifice reveals our authentic character and devotion.

Sermon Title: Rock Church - Easter 2011 by Miles McPherson
Illustration Name: The Atheist Professor and the Marine
Tags: Testimony, Courage, Humour, Marines, Defense of Faith
Story Summary: God is active, alive, and powerful, even when mockers demand immediate, supernatural signs. The ultimate, infallible proof of His existence is not found in cheap, on-demand tricks, but in the historic reality of the Resurrection.

Sermon Title: Rock Church - Easter 2011 by Miles McPherson
Tags: Football, Authority, Confidence, Prediction, Victory
Story Summary: Jesus Christ announced His resurrection in advance to His disciples and Roman authorities. Like DOI Williams, Jesus told death and the Roman guards that He would get up in three days and there was nothing they could do about it, demonstrating His unstoppable power.

Sermon Title: Rock Church - Easter 2011 by Miles McPherson
Illustration Name: Stepping on a Loose Rock in the Mountain Stream
Tags: Childhood, Brother, Stream, Instability, Trust, Rock
Story Summary: The rock looked solid but had an unstable foundation, leading to a fall. Many place their faith in unstable 'rocks' of the world like feelings or human promises. Jesus Christ is the only truly immovable and secure foundation of our salvation.

Sermon Title: Rock Church - Blessed - Part 1, The Money Metaphor by Miles McPherson
Tags: Childhood, Rain, Gutter, Blockage, Flow, Stewardship
Story Summary: God's blessings are ready to pour out on our lives, but we often have blockages (sticks) like greed or fear clogging the flow. Removing these blockages through stewardship lets blessings flow as a pass-through (whoosh) to others, rather than stopping in a cul-de-sac.

Sermon Title: Rock Church - Blessed - Part 1, The Money Metaphor by Miles McPherson
Illustration Name: How Did That Brother Get That Girl?
Tags: Humour, Relationships, Favour, Undeserved Blessing, Sovereignty
Story Summary: Being blessed is experiencing favor and grace far beyond what our own efforts could ever earn. We cannot explain or take credit for God's blessings (like being born in a wealthy country or receiving eternal life); we must simply receive them with humble gratitude.

Sermon Title: Rock Church - Blessed - Part 1, The Money Metaphor by Miles McPherson
Illustration Name: The Three Employees and the Businessman's Wife
Tags: Stewardship, Business, Accountability, Bride, Church, Trust
Story Summary: Jesus is the businessman, His Bride (the Church) is the wife, and we are the employees entrusted with resources. Giving our tithe is returning what belongs to Him to support His Bride. Withholding our giving is withholding from Christ's Bride, for which we will be held accountable.

Sermon Title: Rock Church - Blessed - Part 1, The Money Metaphor by Miles McPherson
Tags: Creation, Nature, Trust, Provision, Stewardship, Worry
Story Summary: The bird trusts God's daily design for provision, completely free of stress or worry. If God takes perfect care of the birds, how much more will He care for His children? Financial worry reveals a lack of faith in our Creator's character.

Sermon Title: Rock Church - What if? - Rock Church 10-Year Anniversary by Miles McPherson
Illustration Name: The CUP Hearing and the Haircut
Tags: Trials, Focus, Commitment, Marriage, Wife, Liberty Station
Story Summary: Pursuing God's vision requires enduring stressful trials and standing firm, even through uncomfortable situations (represented by the haircut). It also highlights the importance of spouse partnership and supporting each other through high-stakes spiritual burdens.

Sermon Title: Rock Church - What if? - Rock Church 10-Year Anniversary by Miles McPherson
Tags: Service, Volunteering, Fire Evacuation, Elders, Fun, Humour
Story Summary: The true power of the church is demonstrated when it serves others in crisis. Ministry is fundamentally relational and down-to-earth; it builds bridges through practical support, fun, and laughter, showing the joy of God's house.

Sermon Title: Rock Church - What if? - Rock Church 10-Year Anniversary by Miles McPherson
Illustration Name: The Mexico City Movie Theater Chase
Tags: Testimony, Fear, Spiritual Warfare, Courage, Devil, Victory
Story Summary: Many Christians live in constant flight from the devil's lies, guilt, and fear, hiding under metaphorical cars. Since greater is He who is in us, we must stop running from ghosts, stand our ground, confront the devil, and walk in victory.

Sermon Title: Rock Church - DO Something - Part 1, The Plan by Miles McPherson
Illustration Name: The Road Trip Competition to New York
Tags: Personal Testimony, NFL, Chargers, Travel, Competition, Journey
Story Summary: Your life is like a journey from the womb to the tomb, and everything in between is your trip. Just as you must make careful preparations when going on a long road trip—such as checking the oil, inflating tires, preparing a first aid kit, and consulting a map—you must do the exact same in your spiritual life. The road of life is long, and we encounter the same kinds of trials and unexpected detours that we do on a cross-country drive. To navigate safely, we must ensure we have made proper spiritual preparations.

Sermon Title: Rock Church - DO Something - Part 1, The Plan by Miles McPherson
Tags: High School, Teacher, Letter of Recommendation, MIT, Abilities, Identity
Story Summary: God has placed qualities, abilities, and potential within us that we do not even realize we have. He has created us for good works that He has prepared in advance. Often, it is during painful situations or when a spiritual authority speaks truth over us that we realize we are much stronger, smarter, wiser, and more patient than we believed. God has already fully prepared and equipped you for everything He has called you to do—you simply have to believe Him.

Sermon Title: Rock Church - DO Something - Part 1, The Plan by Miles McPherson
Tags: AAA, Travel, Map, TripTik, Directions, Obedience
Story Summary: The yellow highlighted line represents God's Word and His commandments. Our primary purpose in life is very simple: obey God and follow His directions. God has already laid out the path and mapped out the route to get us safely from point A to point B. When we deviate from His instructions or stop paying attention, we get spiritually lost. Repentance is the spiritual equivalent of making a U-turn—turning away from our missteps to get right back on God's highlighted path of obedience.

Sermon Title: Rock Church - DO Something - Part 1, The Plan by Miles McPherson
Tags: Missions, Mexico, Disobedience, Discernment, Scam, Shoes
Story Summary: This story illustrates that our job is to obey God, even when His instructions make no sense to our human logic or conflict with our emotions. Because God is all-knowing, He sees the entire picture and knows the hidden motives, while our understanding is highly limited. Walking by faith means trusting God's direction over what we see or feel, knowing that His guidance is always right, even when it appears counterintuitive.

Sermon Title: Rock Church - DO Something - Part 1, The Plan by Miles McPherson
Tags: Football, Chargers, Injury, Monday Night Football, Raiders, Knee Surgery, Witnessing
Story Summary: Collisions and painful trials will happen in life, often coming from 'friendly fire'—our own teammates or loved ones. These events can feel like the end of our dreams and plans. However, pain is not a useless experience. While we cannot always control or understand our pain, we can control how we respond to it. God often uses our brokenness and painful situations to place us in positions where we can be powerful witnesses to others who are trapped next to us, displaying His strength through our weakness.

Sermon Title: Rock Church - DO Something - Part 3, Purpose by Miles McPherson
Illustration Name: Akeelah and the Bee & Crying in Movies
Tags: Wife, Marriage, Movie, Akeelah and the Bee, Crying, Rules, Dr. Larabee, Debbie, Feelings
Story Summary: Just as Dr. Larabee insisted on absolute compliance with his rules to coach Akeelah, God is incredibly stubborn and uncompromising when it comes to His standards. God does not operate a democracy or negotiate His terms; it is His way or no way. If we want His blessing, we must completely submit to His standards and let go of our pride, emotions, and habits. Loving God is not about feelings, but about absolute obedience to His written Word.

Sermon Title: Rock Church - DO Something - Part 3, Purpose by Miles McPherson
Tags: Nature, Orange Tree, Fruit, Salvation, Obedience, Holy Spirit
Story Summary: In the Christian life, we do not perform good works or obey rules in order to become saved (get on the team), but rather, our obedience and good works are the natural 'fruit' of our salvation. The Holy Spirit inside us naturally produces spiritual fruit (love, obedience, change of habits) without us having to gruntingly force it in our own strength. True fruit is evidence of who we are in Christ.

Sermon Title: Rock Church - DO Something - Part 3, Purpose by Miles McPherson
Illustration Name: Jumping on Unstable Rocks in the Stream
Tags: Childhood, Grandparents, New York, Stream, Brother, Rocks, Foundation, Trust
Story Summary: Faith is only as good as the object and foundation it is placed on. When we make decisions based on shifting, unstable things—like our emotions, the culture, or human opinions—we are jumping on unstable rocks and will inevitably fall. God's Word is the only solid, objective fact we can trust. Building our lives on His truth ensures a solid and secure footing.

Sermon Title: Rock Church - DO Something - Part 3, Purpose by Miles McPherson
Tags: Gibbons Monkey, Trap, Coconut, Hunter, Candy, Surrender, Money, Pride
Story Summary: The monkey's captivity and death are entirely caused by its refusal to let go of the candy. Similarly, we often find ourselves trapped and spiritually defeated because we refuse to let go of the worldly things we trust in—whether it's money, physical beauty, intelligence, relationships, or career. To experience God's freedom and blessing, we must open our hands and surrender everything to Him.

Sermon Title: Rock Church - Edge of Darkness - Good Friday by Miles McPherson
Illustration Name: Scaring the Kids & The Nature of Darkness
Tags: Father, Childhood, Scaring, Kids, Darkness, Devil, Strategy, Light
Story Summary: While physical darkness is merely the absence of light, spiritual darkness is a very different and active force. It has a mind, an intent, a leader (the devil), a strategy, and a plan. The devil's goal is to destroy us and bring us to the edge of his darkness, throwing everything he can to pressure us. While the devil cannot force us to sin, he pressures us so intensely that we choose to disqualify ourselves from God's blessings. To defeat spiritual darkness, we must bring the active 'light' of Jesus Christ into our lives.

Illustration Name: The 40-Day Fast in Zero-Degree Marion, Indiana
Tags: Personal Testimony, Fasting, Discipline, Cold, Marion Indiana, Miles Ahead
Story Summary: Fasting is not about starvation; it is about separating yourself from your "perceived" life support (like food or coffee) so you can focus exclusively on your actual Life Support—Jesus Christ. Many people believe a long fast is impossible, but God provides the grace and strength when we submit our physical bodies to Him. By emptying our physical frames, we open ourselves up to be filled and empowered by the Holy Spirit for supernatural tasks.

Tags: Beach, Ocean, Horizon, Visual Metaphor, Perspective, Greatness
Story Summary: This is like measuring God's power and presence in our lives. We only experience a tiny fraction of His power—a mere 14-mile horizon of our own limited perspective. But His power, wisdom, and love are infinite and immeasurable, extending far deeper and wider than we can comprehend. When we pray and fast, we are stepping out into the deep waters of His vast resources, moving past our limited personal horizons.

Tags: Habits, Coffee, Caffeine, Addiction, Withdrawals, Self-Discipline
Story Summary: We frequently spoil ourselves with minor daily comforts and luxuries, such as coffee, without realizing we have formed a deep dependency. When we claim "I gotta have my coffee to get my day started," we are putting a chemical before God. We are relying on caffeine for our daily strength instead of relying on the Holy Spirit. Fasting is designed to expose and break these minor daily addictions, training our flesh to submit to God so that our physical desires do not dictate our spiritual walk.

Tags: Medical, Treadmill, Stress Test, Solitude, Rest, Hectic Life, Devil
Story Summary: This is what the devil does to our lives. He gets us running on a hectic, endless "treadmill" of work, worry, and busyness for years, wearing us into the ground. We are so busy keeping up with the treadmill that we have no time for prayer, solitude, or hearing God's voice. Fasting is God's way of stepping in and turning the treadmill off. It forces us to slow down, rest, and spend time in quiet solitude, realizing that God has everything under control and we don't need to live on the hectic, stressful treadmill of the world.

Illustration Name: Meeting Terry Jackson of the NY Giants
Tags: College, Coach, NFL, Terry Jackson, NY Giants, Relationship, Conversation
Story Summary: We often get incredibly nervous about praying because we view God as an untouchable, distant celebrity, and we don't know how to talk to Him. But prayer is simply a conversation with a Person who loves us and desires a relationship with us. God is not a good-luck charm, a rabbit's foot, or a genie in a bottle; He is a personal God. When we pray, we are having a two-way conversation, where we must speak honestly and, most importantly, quiet our hearts to listen to Him.

Tags: Acronym, AWCIPA, Prayer Model, Discipline, Focus, Alignment
Story Summary: The AWCIPA model is designed to align our hearts with God's will. By focusing on one letter at a time, we learn to order our thoughts, humble ourselves, and listen to God. God already knows what we need before we ask, but prayer is about us getting in line with Him, not trying to get Him in line with us. When we pray through this structure, we learn to desire what He desires, leading to answered prayers and deep spiritual peace.

Sermon Title: The Invisible Man - Part 1, The Invisible Man is Qualified
Illustration Name: The Three Roles of a Construction Project
Tags: Construction, Liberty Station, Architect, Contract, Project Manager, Trinity
Story Summary: This three-role process serves as a metaphor for the Holy Trinity. In the Plan of Salvation for our lives: God the Father is the visionary with the big plan. Jesus Christ is the one who executed the contract on the cross, purchasing our salvation with His blood. The Holy Spirit is the Project Manager, who is on the job inside our hearts every single day, implementing the plan, managing our struggles, performing miracles, and guiding us according to God's specifications.

Sermon Title: The Invisible Man - Part 1, The Invisible Man is Qualified
Tags: Airport, Terminal 2, Glass, Architect, Design, Holy Spirit, Qualifications
Story Summary: The Holy Spirit is highly qualified and has an incredible portfolio. He didn't just build the physical universe; He designed us and wants to construct a beautiful, spiritual temple inside our hearts. When we look at the qualifications and portfolio of the Holy Spirit, we see that He is God, all-powerful, and all-knowing. We must learn to trust His "construction" in our lives because He is more than qualified to remodel our hearts.

Sermon Title: The Invisible Man - Part 1, The Invisible Man is Qualified
Tags: Board Meeting, Heaven, Care, Plans, Calling, Gifts
Story Summary: This illustration highlights that God's care for us is highly personal, detailed, and active. The Trinity is constantly coordinating, planning, and discussing your life, seeking to reveal your unique spiritual gifts, assignments, and calling. You are never forgotten or alone; the very Spirit of God is on the job daily, guiding you into your divine purpose.

Sermon Title: The Invisible Man - Part 1, The Invisible Man is Qualified
Tags: Beach Ball, Universe, Physics, Gravity, Astronomy, Supernatural, Outside the Box
Story Summary: Human beings are trapped inside this "beach ball" and make all their decisions based on its natural, physical laws. However, God exists entirely on the \*outside\* of the beach ball (the supernatural realm) and is not bound by its laws. When the Holy Spirit lives inside us, He brings the supernatural laws and power from \*outside\* the beach ball into our daily lives. This enables us to experience miracles, answers to prayer, and divine provision that completely defy natural math, science, and limitations.

Sermon Title: The Invisible Man - Part 1, The Invisible Man is Qualified
Illustration Name: Getting Cut from the Chargers while Debbie was Pregnant
Tags: Wife, Marriage
Story Summary: Natural math ($500 a month salary vs. $1,500 a month mortgage) did not add up inside the "beach ball." But God is not limited by natural math; He has infinite resources outside the beach ball and was completely faithful to provide for their family. This story demonstrates that even when our security is stripped away and our circumstances seem impossible, we can trust God's provision. He is never caught off guard by our crises and will always provide for His children when we walk in faith.

Tags: Potential, God's perspective, Inner Heart, Faith
Story Summary: God operates like a master photographer: he looks past the surface and assesses our "light potential"—the capacity of our heart to reflect his glory.

Tags: Perception, Senses, Faith, Spiritual Sensitivity
Story Summary: Our natural senses are limited. We must have "subsonic" spiritual hearing—pressing into God to detect his guidance in ways our logical mind cannot perceive.

Illustration Name: Building Campaign at East County (Don't Bet Against God)
Tags: Faith, Boldness, Miracles, God's Glory
Story Summary: God deliberately places us in situations where we are in over our heads, so that when the miracle occurs, he gets 100% of the glory instead of our human wisdom.

Tags: Distractions, Petty Battles, Perspective, Kingdom Focus
Story Summary: The enemy uses trivial arguments and material comparisons (three square feet of dirt) to distract us from fighting the real, spiritual battles of God's massive kingdom.

Tags: Courage, Boldness, Faith, Confronting Giants
Story Summary: We cannot be passive with our giants of fear, addiction, or depression. We must run directly at them, relying on the boldness and victory of Jesus Christ.

Tags: God's Sovereignty, Humour, Trust, Father's Power
Story Summary: We try to solve problems with clever human strategies (parting or walking on water), but God the Father has sovereign, unimaginable ways of directing our "stones" to achieve perfect victory.

Tags: Overcoming Obstacles, Perseverance, Training
Story Summary: Like a trained hurdler, we should see life's obstacles as opportunities to showcase our faith and training.

Tags: Wife, Marriage, Communication, Weakness, Vulnerability
Story Summary: Leadership in relationships requires vulnerability and a willingness to listen to honest feedback about our shortcomings rather than pretending we are perfect.

Tags: Honesty, Restoration, Trust, Fixing Broken Things
Story Summary: A sensitive leader must be like an expert mechanic: willing to walk into a room with highly damaged relationships, speak honest truth, and help put things back together.

Tags: Conviction, Grace, Integrity, Restitution
Story Summary: God's grace convicts us not to condemn us, but to lead us to voluntary repentance and restoration. Even when we steal from him, his love is waiting to welcome us back.

Illustration Name: Hurricane Gustav and Mayor Ray Nagin's Warning
Tags: Dependence on God, Life's Overwhelming Storms, Humility
Story Summary: Trying to face life's overwhelming storms on your own is destructive. Realizing our total dependence on God is key to finding shelter and surviving the storm.

Tags: Perseverance, Resilience, Spiritual Battles, Continuing the Fight
Story Summary: Getting knocked down is guaranteed in life, but with God's strength, you can stand up, shake off the blow, and refuse to quit the spiritual battle.

Tags: Obstacles, Spiritual Blockages, God's Power
Story Summary: God's power is a rushing stream, but we block it with spiritual "sticks" (lack of prayer, Bible study, unrepentant sin). Removing the blocks allows his power to flow.

Tags: Disobedience, Blessings, Consequences, Materialism
Story Summary: When we buy things or enter relationships outside of God's will, we won't enjoy them. God will not bless our disobedience; our material pursuits become sources of torment.

Tags: Respect, Adversity, Hardship, Endurance
Story Summary: Facing adversity requires a relentless, fighting spirit that refuses to go down easily under the enemy's traps.

Tags: Apology, Forgiveness, Reconciliation, Humility, Obedience
Story Summary: True maturity is taking responsibility for our own portion of a conflict, regardless of how wrong the other side was, choosing reconciliation over pride.

Tags: Anger, Grudges, Heart Issues, Displaced Conflict
Story Summary: Our horizontal conflicts with others are often displaced vertical conflicts with God. When we are not right with God, we strike out at those around us.

Illustration Name: The Breastfeeding Mother and the Milk Cows
Tags: Sacrifice, Obedience, Holy Spirit, Supernatural Direction
Story Summary: Walking in the Spirit means going completely against our natural carnal instincts. Obedience often requires us to walk in directions that defy our flesh.

Illustration Name: Flashlight in the Upstate NY Mountains
Tags: Guidance, Faith, God's Word, Next Step
Story Summary: God's Word is a lamp to our feet, not a spotlight on our entire life. He gives us just enough light for the immediate next step, requiring us to walk in faith.

Tags: Nature, Creation, Peace, God's Glory
Story Summary: God speaks through his creation, and the grand scale of the Redwoods serves as a megaphone of his care, reminding us that he can easily sustain us.

Illustration Name: Interceptions, Punt Return, and Humble Pie
Tags: Humility, Pride, Marriage, Expectations, Wife
Story Summary: We often try to impress God or others with our accolades. God serves us humble pie to remind us that he cares about our heart, not our accomplishments.

Tags: Expectations, Misunderstandings, Planning, Faith, Wife, Marriage
Story Summary: We must pray with active expectation. When we meet with God, we should have our Bibles open and a pen ready, expecting him to speak.

Illustration Name: Driving a Stationary Car (The Power of Movement)
Tags: Action, Obedience, Direction, Movement
Story Summary: God cannot steer a parked car. We must start moving in service and obedience, and then God will easily guide and redirect our path.

Tags: Peace, Simplicity, Finding Joy in Small Tasks
Story Summary: Real peace isn't found in worldly achievements, but in slowing down, simplifying our lives, and finding joy in ordinary tasks.

Illustration Name: Standing in the Club to Say "No" to Cocaine
Tags: Temptation, Recommitment, Saying No, Spiritual Strength
Story Summary: Saying no to temptation brings a deep sense of God's smiling approval that far exceeds any physical or worldly pleasure.

Sermon Title: The Ruth Romance - Part 1, Faith Chick Flick
Tags: Gospel, Distress, Hero, Proposal
Story Summary: This is the ultimate metaphor for the Gospel. We are the "bride in distress." Before we accept Christ, we are sinners doomed to hell with no ability to save ourselves. Jesus is our ultimate Hero. He rode into human history, overcame the massive obstacle of the cross and death, paid for our sins, rose again, and offered us a proposal of relationship. When we accept His proposal, we are saved from our desperate situation.

Sermon Title: The Ruth Romance - Part 1, Faith Chick Flick
Illustration Name: March of the Penguins Father Huddle
Tags: Devotion, Endurance, Covenant, Parenting
Story Summary: This is an incredible picture of the covenant and sacrificial commitment required in relationships. True love stands in the freezing storm, enduring severe hardship, starvation, and extreme elements to protect the next generation and fulfill a promise.

Sermon Title: The Ruth Romance - Part 1, Faith Chick Flick
Tags: Wife, Marriage, Friends, Separation, Boundaries
Story Summary: Giving our lives to God and pursuing His plan often means being willing to separate ourselves from close relationships and old friends who lead us back into sin. Our relationship with Jesus (our Groom) must become more important than any human relationship, even if it requires difficult boundaries or separation.

Sermon Title: The Ruth Romance - Part 2, Romanceable Ruth
Tags: Fiero, Freeway, Dog, Big Picture, Trust
Story Summary: In our relationships and life decisions, we often get in trouble because we focus only on what is immediately in front of us—our current feelings, desires, and immediate circumstances. We make frantic, short-sighted choices that lead to emotional crash-ups. God, however, sits at the top of the hill and sees the entire big picture of our lives. We must trust His bird's-eye perspective and follow His rules instead of relying on our narrow, localized vision.

Sermon Title: The Ruth Romance - Part 2, Romanceable Ruth
Tags: Wife, Marriage, Cotton Mouth, Submission, Service
Story Summary: True biblical headship and leadership in a marriage are not about demanding authority or forcing a spouse to "bow down." It is about mutual, self-sacrificing service. To get your spouse to respect and serve you, you must be the first to bow down, serve her, anticipate her fears, and protect her. Mutual submission and love are the keys to a flourishing, Christ-centered marriage.

Sermon Title: The Ruth Romance - Part 2, Romanceable Ruth
Illustration Name: The Grumpy Wife's Three-Year Baby Grace Period
Tags: Wife, Marriage, Baby, Grace, Household Chores
Story Summary: Christ-like love means actively stepping in to serve and support our partners during seasons of intense pressure and exhaustion, without expecting anything in return. We are called to be the chief servants in our homes, reflecting how Christ served and laid His life down for the church.

Sermon Title: Image is Everything, Part 1, The Image Key
Tags: Declaration, Bush, Iraq War, Spoken Word
Story Summary: If the spoken word of a mere human leader carries such immense, country-shaping power, how much more powerful is the voice and Word of God? When God speaks, His words carry creative, redemptive, and transformative power. When His words are spoken into our lives, they act as the ultimate "key" that ignites our true individual identity, shapes our morality, and sets our calling in motion.

Sermon Title: Image is Everything, Part 1, The Image Key
Illustration Name: The Halloween Mask at the Window
Tags: Halloween, Mask, Prank, Hide-and-Seek, Comfort Zone
Story Summary: This is a picture of how God calls us out of our comfortable, well-lit comfort zones. To grow in faith, God often tells us to leave behind the familiar and step into the unfamiliar, pitch-black areas of life where we must rely completely on His voice. We must trust Him as we walk in the dark, calling out "Dad" (our Heavenly Father), knowing He is there to guide and grow us through the unfamiliar.

Sermon Title: Rock Church - The Promise by Miles McPherson
Illustration Name: Chris Henry and the Cincinnati Bengals
Tags: Divine Appointment, NFL, Bengals, Chris Henry, Legacy
Story Summary: We frequently judge people by their failures and worst chapters. But God's grace continually reaches out to people, even to the last second of their lives, because of His ultimate Promise. Chris was walking in a new direction and turning his life around when his life was cut short. God keeps His promise to seek out the lost, love us despite our "junk," and give everyone an opportunity to be restored.

Sermon Title: Rock Church - The Promise by Miles McPherson
Tags: Barren Women, Old Testament, Mary, Virgin Birth, Miracles, Sarah
Story Summary: All of these miraculous Old Testament births prepared the world for the ultimate supernatural birth: Mary's virgin birth. Mary was a young virgin who had never been with a man, and her pregnancy was biologically impossible. Yet, the Holy Spirit overshadowed her, establishing that with God, absolutely nothing is impossible. Just as God has been faithful to perform the impossible through barren women throughout history, we can trust Him to do the impossible in our lives.

Sermon Title: Lord of the Things - Part 1, Getting in the Fast Lane
Tags: Nursery Rhyme, Humpty Dumpty, Fall, False Comforts, Restoration
Story Summary: When our lives fracture and fall apart, we desperately reach out to "the king's men" (therapists, self-help books, career advice, support groups) to put ourselves back together. These resources are helpful but ultimately inadequate. Real restoration is only possible when we bypass intermediate sources and go directly to the King of Kings, establishing His personal lordship over our broken lives.

Sermon Title: Lord of the Things - Part 1, Getting in the Fast Lane
Tags: Physiology, Health, Harvard, Blood, Temple, Holy Spirit
Story Summary: Our physical body is not our own; it is the holy temple of the living God. Stewardship means keeping the temple physically and mentally clean so that we are optimal "vessels" for His service. If our tissues are sluggish and clogged due to toxic food and a sedentary lifestyle, we are hindering the physical instrument God wants to use for His kingdom.

Sermon Title: Lord of the Things - Part 2, Lordship Comedy
Tags: Rancho Peñasquitos, Pregnancy, Selective Compliance, Hypocrisy
Story Summary: Lordship is an all-or-nothing reality, just like pregnancy. You cannot selectively choose which commandments to obey and which to ignore while calling Jesus your "Lord." Half-hearted, selective obedience is actually complete disobedience. True lordship requires complete, absolute submission of every area of our lives to His authority.

Sermon Title: Lord of the Things - Part 2, Lordship Comedy
Tags: Beach, Sand, Foundation, False Security, Storms
Story Summary: This is a picture of building our lives on "sand" (worldly comfort, money, status, relationships). It may seem secure on a sunny day, but as life's waves sweep over us, we sink deeper and deeper into instability. If our foundation is not firmly set on the "Rock" of Jesus Christ through active obedience, our life's house will inevitably crash when the massive storms of life hit.

Sermon Title: Lord of the Things - Part 3, Lordship Lets Go
Tags: Gibbons Monkey, Trap, Coconut, Candy, Surrender, Money, Pride
Story Summary: This is the ultimate picture of how we clench our hands around our "possessions" (money, bad habits, toxic relationships, pride). We are trapped and spiritually defeated, screaming in pain as the enemy closes in on us, entirely because we refuse to let go of the "candy" in our hands. Lordship means opening our hands, surrendering everything we cling to, and letting God have absolute control.

Sermon Title: Lord of the Things - Part 3, Lordship Lets Go
Tags: Rocky, Coma, Adrian, Rematch, Training, Perseverance, Victory
Story Summary: Many Christians complain that living a holy life and being completely obedient to God's standard is "too hard" or impossible. But God has complete confidence in us, and He has given us His Holy Spirit to empower us. When we feel exhausted and ready to quit, God looks at us and says, "Win!" He provides the strength, grace, and authority to overcome every spiritual battle.

Sermon Title: Leave it to Cleave Her, Part 1
Tags: Relationships, Driving, Danger
Story Summary: The driver crashed because she was reacting only to what was immediately in front of her instead of scanning the wider environment. Similarly, in relationships, reacting only to immediate desires without looking at the bigger picture of God's design leads to relational wreckage.

Sermon Title: Leave it to Cleave Her, Part 1
Tags: Purity, Culture, Law
Story Summary: Relational and sexual boundaries are not arbitrary social conventions; they are absolute rules established by God, who designed human nature and marriage. We must choose whether to align our lives with God's word or the shifting standards of the world.

Sermon Title: Leave it to Cleave Her, Part 1
Tags: Temptation, Children
Story Summary: Physical attraction to the opposite sex is a natural, God-given drive. However, because our culture weaponizes sex to sell products and promote lust, we must actively guard our eyes and hearts by aligning with God's pure boundaries.

Sermon Title: Leave it to Cleave Her, Part 1
Tags: Desire, Children
Story Summary: The desire for companionship and romantic love is normal and healthy. However, we must be careful to choose partners who are spiritually and emotionally whole, rather than settling for hollow, superficial "torsos" that are falling apart.

Sermon Title: Leave it to Cleave Her, Part 1
Tags: Design, Control
Story Summary: God placed our most critical bodily functions on "autopilot" to protect us. However, He gave us full manual control over our relationships, sex life, and moral choices. Since these areas are not on autopilot, they require absolute obedience to God's instructions, or we will cause devastating damage.

Sermon Title: Leave it to Cleave Her, Part 2
Tags: Wife, Marriage, Protection, Discernment
Story Summary: God designed the wife from the man's rib to walk by his side and protect his heart. A wife's spiritual discernment is a vital protective shield for the marriage and family; husbands must value and trust their wives' discernment to guard their hearts.

Sermon Title: Leave it to Cleave Her, Part 2
Tags: Culture, Idolatry
Story Summary: Marital union brings oneness, but true wholeness and spiritual completeness only come from a union with Jesus Christ. No human being can fill the void in your soul; placing that expectation on a spouse leads to disappointment and idolatry.

Tags: Prison, Future, Forgiveness
Story Summary: God doesn't look at us with grief over our past sins or failures, but with excitement for what we can become from this day forward when we surrender our lives to Him.

Tags: Service, Palm Sunday, Christ
Story Summary: We are like the donkey. We can never be God, but if we carry Jesus and let Him lead us, we get to experience the miraculous and amazing things He does. People might even give us credit, but we must always remember that we are just the donkey carrying the King.

Illustration Name: Admitting You are Wrong in Marriage
Tags: Wife, Marriage, Pride, Communication
Story Summary: Christ sets us free from our pride and the bondage of our egos. True freedom in Christ means having the humility to say, "I was wrong," to repent, and to offer or receive forgiveness.

Tags: Daughter, Newborn, Parents
Story Summary: We belong to God, and He watches over us with the same intense, loving pride of a father looking at his newborn child. He is excited about our specific purpose, even with our soft-headed "points" and imperfections.

Illustration Name: Hepatitis C Ink and Wet Rag
Tags: Prison, Sickness, Pride
Story Summary: The Bible says our self-righteousness is like filthy rags (Isaiah 64:6) - dirty, contaminated, and potentially poisonous. We cannot present our own good works to God as righteousness; we must be clothed in the perfect righteousness that Christ provides.

Tags: Foundation, Youth, Childhood
Story Summary: In a world of shifting sand and muddy pits, God takes our feet out of the "miry clay" and places them on a Rock-solid foundation (Psalm 40:2). Our steps are established when we are anchored to Christ, the Rock of our salvation.

Tags: Preschool, Youth, Humor
Story Summary: Developing meaningful relationships across generations—both with the elderly and with children—is essential for a rich life. It reminds us to constantly evaluate if our actions and the places we go please God.

Sermon Title: Salvation in the Days of Noah
Tags: Sickness, Balance, Hearing
Story Summary: Every day, our spirit tries to line up God's will with our own. When our spiritual receptors (our "stereocilia") are clogged by sin, distraction, or a bad attitude, we receive a distorted signal. We must align ourselves with God's truth to regain our spiritual balance, especially when coming to church with a bad attitude.

Sermon Title: Salvation in the Days of Noah
Tags: Ark, Stats, Flood
Story Summary: God's instructions and provisions are incredibly practical and mathematically precise, even when they seem impossible or "crazy" to the world. When God calls us to a task, His provision is more than sufficient to preserve us and carry us through the storm.

Sermon Title: Salvation in the Days of Noah
Tags: Science, Fossils, Flood
Story Summary: The physical evidence of God's cataclysmic judgment is visible all around us in the earth. The flood is a real historical warning of judgment, but it also highlights the reality of God's salvation and covenant for those who believe.

Sermon Title: Salvation in the Days of Noah
Illustration Name: PBS Journey of Sperm and Egg
Tags: Biology, Birth, Children
Story Summary: This microscopic, highly complex process is scientifically undeniable yet mathematically impossible to attribute to random chance. It is a supernatural design. We must realize that our very existence is a miracle of God. When we face difficulties, we must thank God that we even survived the womb and are alive today.

Sermon Title: Salvation in the Days of Noah
Tags: Sickness, Cross, Perspective
Story Summary: Our daily complaints and problems are trivial and highly fixable compared to the agony Christ suffered to save us. Keeping our eyes on the cross eliminates self-pity and fills us with gratitude.

Tags: Skills, Diving, Shark
Story Summary: Extraordinary dreams require extraordinary skills. Just as diving in deep water requires mastery of specific survival skills beforehand, Joseph's dream only came true because he spent years in Egypt perfecting his skills in management and dream interpretation. We must identify and sharpen our God-given strengths.

Tags: Football, Sports, Hard Times
Story Summary: In life and ministry, we cannot avoid obstacles, confrontations, or hard times. Instead of fearing them, we must welcome collisions as God's training ground to sharpen us, build our endurance, and prepare us for the next level.

Tags: Pride, Discipline, Correction
Story Summary: Our hard-headed pride and arrogance cannot be smoothed out by gentle means. God uses circumstances and other people—rough, abrasive tools—to grind down our rough edges. Just as "iron sharpens iron," we need other people to speak the truth in love and keep us sharp.

Tags: Preaching, MLK, Passion
Story Summary: Fulfilling a God-given dream requires absolute passion and fire. We must be so aligned with God's purpose that we are consumed by it, working diligently even when no one is looking.

Tags: Speech, Discipline, Humility
Story Summary: The process of training, refining your communication, and receiving critique is incredibly humbling. However, if we refuse to undergo the humbling process of feedback, we will never achieve the excellence required to stand before kings and fulfill our calling.

Illustration Name: The Billy Graham Secret Service Bullet Shield
Tags: Sacrifice, Protection, Jesus
Story Summary: This is a physical picture of Jesus Christ's protection and ultimate sacrifice. Christ took the "bullet" of sin and death for us on the cross, taking our place so that we could be saved and shielded from judgment.

Tags: Identity, Misunderstanding
Story Summary: We often carry preconceived, incorrect ideas about who we are and what our names and purposes mean. But just as the comedian's grandma defined "Nana Pudding," God defines our true identity, name, and calling.

Illustration Name: The Closet Escape and the Realities of Motherhood
Tags: Wife, Marriage, Family, Children, Sacrifice
Story Summary: Husbands and families must recognize and honor the mental load, deep sacrifices, and constant responsibilities that mothers carry daily. True love and intimacy require sharing those burdens rather than adding to them.

Illustration Name: The Duck and the 10 Chicks on a 50 mph Road
Tags: Protection, Guidance, Dependency
Story Summary: This is a beautiful picture of Jesus' longing to gather us under His wings to protect us from the dangerous, high-speed elements of this world. Just as chicks survive by staying close in line behind their mother, we must stay dependent on and close behind Christ to remain safe under His care.

Illustration Name: The 21-Week-Old Fetus Grabbing the Surgeon's Finger
Tags: Creation, Love, Value, Sanctity of Life
Story Summary: This illustrates that our lives are supernaturally formed and known by God from the womb. We are not accidents; we are fearfully and wonderfully made by a personal Creator who knows us intimately and feels our pain.

Tags: Faith, Risk, Supernatural, Slang
Story Summary: God constantly challenges us to step out of our safe comfort zones and exercise "Baducka Faith" so that He can demonstrate His supernatural power.

Tags: Faithfulness, Running, Buffalo, Provision, Doubt
Story Summary: When facing massive, seemingly impossible giants, we must remember and honor God's past victories in our lives. If He delivered us from the "lion and the bear" in the past, He will deliver us from the current giant.

Illustration Name: John Kitna and the Cincinnati Bengals Turnaround
Tags: Football, Bengals, NFL, Calling, Platform
Story Summary: When we focus entirely on our God-given calling and purpose, God can take our circumstances from "last place" to "first place" in an instant. Our platform (our job, sport, or business) is not our ultimate purpose; it is merely the vehicle God uses to let us represent Him.

Tags: Compromise, Obedience, Relationships, Confrontation
Story Summary: Walking with God requires active obedience and alignment with His commands, not just verbal professions. True repentance means turning away from compromise and returning to God's standard.

Illustration Name: The Deaf Girl in Mexico and Pastor Mike McIntosh
Story Summary: In 1984, as a 24-year-old rookie defensive back with the Chargers, Miles McPherson attended team chapel. The guest speaker was Pastor Mike McIntosh, who arrived casually dressed in blue jeans and a Hawaiian shirt. McIntosh shared a story about a little deaf girl in Mexico who had her hearing miraculously restored after he prayed for her. This story moved Miles to tears and eventually led him to join Horizon Christian Fellowship, where he served on staff for 15 years.

A close friend of Miles felt prompted by the Lord to pick up a homeless man walking slowly in pouring rain in La Mesa. Initially resisting, the friend drove home but returned after the prompt persisted. The passenger, who was missing one eye, immediately praised the Lord and spent the entire 5-mile drive sharing deep spiritual insights. After being dropped off at a busy intersection, the man instantly vanished.

Illustration Name: The Nutcracker Ballet and the Girls' Angels
Story Summary: While attending his daughter's Nutcracker performance at the Spreckels Theater, Miles sat behind a group of talkative eight-year-old girls. An observer (a schoolteacher) looked at him with extreme suspicion, making Miles feel self-conscious. Once Miles explained he was a choir member at the Rock Church, the teacher warmly recognized him. Miles noted that every child has a ministering angel that reports to the Father.

Miles called a friend but dialed the wrong number. The man who answered asked who it was, and when Miles identified himself, the man gasped, explaining he had attended Miles' Christmas Eve service four years ago and hadn't been back since. Miles prayed with him over his current struggles and invited him back for the upcoming Christmas Eve service, which fell on the exact four-year anniversary.

Illustration Name: The Phabulous Pharisees and Leather Boxes
Story Summary: Miles explains the historical character of the Pharisees, who wore special robes and leather straps with small boxes (phylacteries) containing Scripture on their foreheads and wrists. This was an outward, showy sign of holiness. Miles jokes that this is the equivalent of a modern pastor strapping a giant physical Bible to his head with duct tape.

Miles admits that being "Pastor Miles" 24/7 is a heavy and exhausting burden, and sometimes he just wants to be "Miles." He notes that coaching youth football is a major blessing because the players and coaches treat him simply as "Coach," allowing him to drop his public guard and find a temporary sanctuary.

During a pizza party at Pizza Hut after a game, Miles watched the youth cheerleaders. Throughout the season, he had admired their flawless, perfectly bouncy curls. He was shocked when several cheerleaders walked past his table holding their "perfect curls" in their hands, revealing they were fake clip-on hairpieces.

During his college years, Miles challenged himself to run a grueling, hilly 2-3 mile course in New Haven with the varsity baseball team. Suffering from exercise-induced asthma, Miles faced intense physical agony when the team's leader took off at a full sprint. Refusing to lose to a baseball player, Miles pressed through the burning pain to finish tied for first.

Sermon Title: Why Bad Things Happen To Good People - Part 1
Tags: Wife, Marriage, Children, Parenting, Discipline
Story Summary: This is how God often deals with our trials. When we experience initial pain or a setback, our first reaction is fear, panic, and seeking attention. God doesn't always rush to instantly rescue us; He lets us process our fear, teaching us to grow strong and resilient through minor injuries.

Sermon Title: Why Bad Things Happen To Good People - Part 1
Tags: Sickness, Pain, Perseverance
Story Summary: Many of the "monstrous" problems we complain about are just re-opened old emotional scabs from our past (a broken relationship, a past insult, a minor failure). We blow on them and act like our life is over, but God looks at us and says, "Get up, shake it off, this is a normal part of life. You have the strength to handle it."

Sermon Title: Why Bad Things Happen To Good People - Part 1
Illustration Name: The "Curse God to His Face" Agenda
Tags: Satan, Temptation, Battle
Story Summary: Every major problem in our lives—losing a job, getting a disease, going through a breakup—is weaponized by the enemy for one purpose: to get us to curse God, doubt His love, and walk away from Him. The devil wants us to believe that God is a liar or doesn't care. Our battle is not against the physical problem (the job, the boss, the sickness); the battle is to remain firmly under God's protective wings and refuse to doubt His goodness.

Sermon Title: Why Bad Things Happen To Good People - Part 1
Tags: Sickness, Hardship, Trial, Perspective
Story Summary: Life's trials often hit us in overwhelming, rapid-fire succession—one bad news report after another, leaving us dazed. The enemy times these blows perfectly to crush our faith. But like Job, we must respond to the rapid-fire bad news by falling to our knees and saying, "The Lord gave, and the Lord has taken away; blessed be the name of the Lord."

Sermon Title: Why Bad Things Happen to Good People - Part 4
Illustration Name: The Pleiades and Orion Light Year Scale
Tags: Creation, Stars, Sovereignty, Perspective
Story Summary: We get consumed with our minor, local problems (unpaid bills, minor insults, personal worries) as if they are the center of the universe. God points us to the cosmos He constructed with his fingers to remind us of His infinite, mind-boggling scale. If He can manage stars light-years away, He can easily handle our small local problems.

Sermon Title: Why Bad Things Happen to Good People - Part 4
Illustration Name: The 10-Foot Bald Eagle and the Broken Arm
Tags: Creation, Nature, Design, Strength
Story Summary: This is a picture of God's incredible, majestic design in creation. God designed the eagle's claws, its eyes to see a fish from a mile away, and its wings to glide on wind currents. When we look at the power and detail of His creation, we realize we can trust Him with the intricate details of our lives.

Sermon Title: Why Bad Things Happen to Good People - Part 4
Illustration Name: The Woodpecker's Head-Shock Absorber and Wrap-Around Tongue
Tags: Creation, Design, Sickness, Protection
Story Summary: Every single creature, no matter how small, is meticulously engineered with perfect, supernatural mechanisms to survive and thrive. This is not the result of random evolution. If God spent so much detail designing a woodpecker's tongue and shock-absorbing head, how much more care has He put into designing, protecting, and preserving your life through hard times.

Tags: Faith, Healing, Miracles, Trust
Story Summary: This physical, biological healing was an undeniable miracle of flesh and bone. Miles uses it to show that if God has the supernatural power to physically remodel a little girl's ears, we can completely trust Him to repair and resolve any complex, seemingly impossible life problems we face.

Tags: Apologetics, Creation, Evolution, Design
Story Summary: Yet, our culture readily accepts that human beings—who are infinitely more complex than a watch, with eyes, brains, lungs, and the capacity to think, feel, and love—are the result of random evolutionary chance. We are meticulously designed by a Master Creator, and denying His design is intellectually dishonest.

Illustration Name: The "Shut Up to Your Father" Lesson
Tags: Family, Parenting, Consequence, Judgment
Story Summary: Every action in life has an immediate "therefore"—an inescapable consequence or result. If we choose to live in disobedience to God, we cannot escape the "therefore" of His spiritual laws and eventual judgment. We must recognize the severity of our rebellion and repent.

Illustration Name: Chuck Norris and the POW Rat
Tags: Hell, Judgment, Severity
Story Summary: As intense, brutal, and terrifying as earthly warfare and torture can be, it is absolutely nothing compared to the severity of the eternal judgment of God. In hell, there is weeping and gnashing of teeth with no rest, which is why we must urgently share the gospel and lead people to repentance.

Illustration Name: The Malvern Mules Blow-Pop Pep Rally
Tags: Youth, Humility, Identity, Purpose
Story Summary: Miles entered the gym feeling proud but rode out looking like a fool. In contrast, the donkey that carried Jesus into Jerusalem on Palm Sunday entered as an obscure, unused animal but rode out as a hero because it was set apart to carry the King of Kings. God wants to take us out of our obscurity and old lifestyles, set us apart, and use us for the greatest purposes in His Kingdom.

Illustration Name: Mission to Mars and the Hometown Buffet
Tags: Wife, Marriage, Family
Story Summary: The secular world invents wild evolutionary theories to avoid admitting accountability to a Creator. Similarly, people try to satisfy their inner emptiness by stuffing themselves with worldly pleasures at the "buffet of life," leaving them bloated and spiritually starving. Only a personal relationship with Jesus satisfies the soul.

Tags: Youth, Catholicism, Holiness
Story Summary: In Greek, "holy" means to be set apart for one specific purpose. Just as ordinary water is made "holy" by being set aside strictly for baptism, God wants to cleanse us of our sinful pasts, set us apart from the world, and make us holy vessels dedicated exclusively to His divine service.

Tags: Surrender, Stewardship, Obedience
Story Summary: Jesus does not need to own worldly goods because He owns everything. Today, He wants to "borrow" your life, your home, your car, your talents, and your heart for His work. When we reluctantly withhold our resources, time, or finances from Him, we are fighting against the very purpose for which He set us apart.
